title: DWS Batch Authorization and Login Recommend Flow

context.problem: DWS login --recommend and product-line batch authorization need a user-visible authorization page instead of silent bulk grants or local --yes-only blocking.

larkCli.yesFlag: lark-cli auth login has no --yes flag; lark-cli auth login --yes --no-wait --json returns Error: unknown flag: --yes. DWS intentionally differs because --yes is an inherited batch-auto-confirm flag: dws auth login --recommend --yes must skip the browser confirmation flow and grant the service-selected scopes directly.

ralphCli.model: PRD-driven long-running development with explicit task ledger, progress notes, verification gates, and failure analysis.
ralphCli.mapping: This PRD keeps a single SSOT for goals, contracts, tasks, security gates, acceptance criteria, and blocked evidence so implementation can continue across multiple repositories without redefining done.

reference.targetPage: Reuse the personalAuthorization route and render multiple scopes grouped by product.

accessNotes: The frontend GitLab repo was cloned through SSH to /Users/xuan/lippi-open-dev-front and implemented on branch codex/batch-auth-page-flow.
The DWS PAT BFF lives in /Users/xuan/lippi-open-app-dev and needs to be part of the chain because it owns /dingtalk-workspace-cli/oauth/device/action.
Acceptance is not complete after local build. It is complete only after prepub package/build succeeds for every changed repo and prepub functional verification passes.
The referenced DingTalk minutes URL could not be fetched because local dws is not logged in.

goals: After dws auth login --recommend succeeds, DWS starts a PAT recommended batch plan and opens the authorization page.

For PAT batch authorization commands through dws pat chmod without --yes, including multi-scope, product-line, and recommend modes, DWS opens the same authorization page instead of returning a local validation error.
The server, not the CLI, selects grantable scopes. CLI must grant only selectedScopes from pat.batch_plan.

Recommended login scope selection is controlled by prepub Diamond: open-source visible scopes are the enabled template set, recommendation is the low-risk subset, and the 22 medium-risk scopes are excluded from default login recommend.
High-risk scopes remain pending and must not be silently granted.
nonGoals: Do not trust CLI-provided orgId or uid.
Do not add a second authorization UI path.
Do not make medium-risk or high-risk destructive scopes part of default login recommend.

userFlows.name: loginRecommend
steps: User runs dws auth login --recommend.
CLI completes OAuth loopback or device login, lets the user choose the DingTalk organization through the normal login flow, and saves the token.
After the login identity and organization are settled, interactive CLI shows the Feishu-like terminal authorization guide: 选择操作, then 授权范围选择.
CLI calls pat.batch_plan with recommend=true by default, or recommend=false when the user selected 全部授权, grantType=permanent, caller=auth_login_recommend, dryRun=true.
CLI then shows the product-domain selector with all product lines selected by default; user can toggle, filter, or confirm.
CLI re-plans with the selected productCodes before starting the grant flow.
PAT core loads the Diamond-driven recommended set, excludes non-recommended medium-risk scopes from the default plan, and returns selectedScopes plus warnings/skipped scopes.
CLI calls pat.batch_grant with scopes=selectedScopes, startFlow=true, noWait=true, caller=auth_login_recommend.
PAT core creates a single batch Device Code Flow and returns PAT_BATCH_AUTH_PENDING with flowId/userCode/uri.
CLI opens uri, polls flow status, and retries the original grant after approval.

userFlows.name: batchChmodWithoutYes
steps: User runs dws pat chmod --recommend --product <product> without --yes.
CLI plans first, then starts a batch Device Code Flow instead of direct grant.
The authorization page displays all grantable selected scopes grouped by product.
Approve writes selected grants; reject leaves all grants unchanged.

userFlows.name: batchChmodWithYes
steps: User runs the same command with --yes.
CLI continues the existing non-interactive grant path.
PAT core writes grantable low/medium-risk scopes directly and returns high-risk scopes as pending.

larkCliParity.serverContract: pat.batch_plan recommend=true caller=auth_login_recommend, then pat.batch_grant selectedScopes startFlow=true noWait=true
larkCliParity.status: implemented locally in CLI, gateway, PAT core, BFF, and frontend; prepub package and verification pending

larkCliParity.serverContract: pat.batch_plan productCodes=[...] resolves service-owned product scope templates
larkCliParity.status: implemented for PAT chmod product-line flow

larkCliParity.serverContract: one scope preserves direct grant behavior; multiple scopes enter batch plan/flow when --yes is absent
frontend sends selectedScopes; PAT core rejects any selected scope outside flow scopes and treats unchecked flow scopes as rejectedScopes
larkCliParity.status: implemented locally in PAT core, BFF selectedScopes passthrough, and frontend grouped selection; prepub verification pending

larkCliParity.dws: PAT_BATCH_AUTH_PENDING JSON with uri/authUrl/flowId/userCode plus polling retry
larkCliParity.serverContract: host-owned JSON mode returns structured pending and does not block; local interactive mode opens browser and polls
larkCliParity.status: implemented in CLI retry path and PAT pending response

larkCliParity.dws: supported only as dws auth login --recommend --yes; inherited --yes skips local TUI and web confirmation and directly grants the PAT-core selected safe recommend scopes
larkCliParity.serverContract: auth login --recommend without --yes must still open or return the authorization page. With --yes, the CLI omits startFlow/noWait and relies on PAT core to grant only service-selected safe scopes; server-side pending/high-risk/page-required responses still win.
larkCliParity.status: verified on 2026-06-16 with local lark-cli: unknown flag --yes; DWS --yes semantics are product-specific and intentionally bypass the web confirmation page for recommend direct grant

larkCliParity.dws: pat.batch_plan dryRun and existing PAT auth check result
larkCliParity.serverContract: plan exposes selected/skipped/pending/already-granted scope sets; check/auth retry carries missing scope and grant options
larkCliParity.status: partially aligned; no new public scope-catalog command added in this slice

tasks.title: Build PRD and command parity map
tasks.description: Use product-design style requirements plus Ralph-style SSOT to map DWS batch authorization against lark-cli.
steps: Define user flows for login recommend, product-line grant, multi-scope grant, yes direct grant, and JSON no-wait mode.
Record frontend access and DingTalk minutes access blockers explicitly.
Keep acceptance criteria tied to command outputs and service contracts.

tasks.title: Implement DWS CLI batch authorization entry points
tasks.description: dws auth login --recommend and dws pat chmod batch/product/recommend without --yes start the page-based flow.
steps: Add --recommend to auth login and run PAT recommended plan after login.
Route product-line, recommend, and multi-scope chmod without --yes to pat.batch_grant startFlow.
Keep --yes as the explicit direct-write path.

tasks.title: Harden gateway identity and caller allowlist
tasks.description: The gateway must derive orgId/uid from trusted identity and allow intended DWS batch callers.
steps: Keep CLI-provided orgId/uid untrusted.
Allow dws-cli alongside openclaw for intended batch write callers.
Preserve caller/source checks for default grant writes.

tasks.title: Implement PAT core recommended filtering and batch DCF
tasks.description: PAT core owns selectedScopes, Diamond-driven recommendation, batch Device Code Flow, selected approval, and idempotent writes.
steps: Keep recommended scope selection config-driven: preauth Diamond recommends the low-risk subset and excludes the 22 medium-risk scopes.
Create/reuse one batch flow for selected scopes.
Approve only selectedScopes and reject out-of-flow selections.
Fail closed on critical storage failures and leave flow retryable.

tasks.title: Implement lippi-open-dev-front batch authorization UI
tasks.description: Reuse /src/app/personalAuthorization/index.jsx and render grouped product permissions with selectable scopes.
steps: Load flow detail and detect multiple scopes.
Default-check all server-selected flow scopes.
Allow product and individual-scope deselection.
Submit selectedScopes on approve and no orgId/uid.
Handle PAT_BATCH_PARTIAL_FAILURE by keeping the page retryable.

tasks.title: Submit all repos to prepub and verify package plus behavior
tasks.description: Acceptance only passes after prepub package/build succeeds and prepub functional verification is OK.
steps: Push or submit branches for CLI, gateway, PAT core, BFF, and frontend.
Trigger prepub package/build for every changed repo.
Verify prepub deployed versions include the current change set.
Run login recommend and batch chmod functional checks on prepub.
Open the prepub authorization page and verify layout and approve/reject callbacks.

latestDiagnostics: PAT core Aone compile error at PatAuthCheckService.java:517 was caused by a two-argument tryConsumeOnceTokenFromTair call after the method signature changed to require effective risk level. Current feature and release-merge worktrees use RiskLevelEnum.MEDIUM.getCode() for the high-risk-to-medium DCF bypass path and compile locally.

The Maven warning about requested profile lippi-pat-core is an Aone/Maven parameter warning: the root POM has no profile with id lippi-pat-core. It is not the cause of the Java compilation failure and does not justify a POM change in this slice.

PAT core API version is explicitly aligned: root property lippi-pat-core-api.version is 1.0.5-SNAPSHOT and lippi-pat-core-api/pom.xml project.version evaluates to 1.0.5-SNAPSHOT. Verification passed with Java 11 Maven help:evaluate and lippi-pat-core-api compile.

PAT core Aone build task 3390400102 failed because release branch commit 3555c3aa resolved lippi-open-app-dev-api as 1.0.103, which lacks PatConnectorQueryService and PatConnectorQueryResult. Local Java 11 lippi-pat-core-service compile passed and the 1.2.0-SNAPSHOT jar contains both missing classes.

PAT core prepub runtime failure for flowId 8b35d5b6373c4cb9a7a67b339516fff2 was TDDL-4603 ERR_ACCROSS_DB_TRANSACTION. The failing path approved a batch flow by looping single-scope INSERT INTO agent_pat in one transaction, crossing sharding groups. Commit 0b805ff changes permanent/session batch approval and direct batch grant to PatAuthGrantService#grantScopesBatch, which uses AgentPatDAO.batchInsert and PatAuthRecordDAO.batchInsert where applicable, while DeviceCodeFlowService#handleFlowAction runs with Propagation.NOT_SUPPORTED to avoid opening a strict cross-shard transaction around the flow action.

response.warnings: recommendation filter and partial selection warnings

request.scopes: required string[], must be selectedScopes from plan

request.grantType: optional once|session|permanent; batch page omits it so PAT core uses defaultGrantType from the flow
request.selectedScopes: required for the new batch page when action=approve; must be a non-empty subset of flow.scopes

compatibility.selectedScopesNull: legacy clients approve all flow-stored scopes

response.approvedScopes: scopes written by this approve action
response.rejectedScopes: flow scopes not approved by this action

errorBehavior.approveStorageFailure: PAT_BATCH_PARTIAL_FAILURE; flow remains PENDING and the page can retry. Once tokens written by the current flow are cleaned by flowId ownership; permanent/session grants rely on transaction rollback and grant idempotency.

frontendRequirements: Reuse personalAuthorization page and API detail/action callbacks.
When scopes length is greater than one, render a grouped batch authorization surface.
Group by productCode/productName with product-level checkbox and expandable scope details.
Default checked state is all safe selected scopes.
Users can uncheck a product group or individual scopes; approving with zero selected scopes is disabled.
Approve must send selectedScopes matching the current checked scope list.
Do not expose orgId or uid as trusted client input.

visualDesignTarget.page: A white authorization card centered on a light #f5f6fa page, with the footer links below the card.
visualDesignTarget.card: Single white 480x680 desktop card with rgba(131,137,143,0.16) border, no shadow, and 12px radius.
visualDesignTarget.header: Top centered dynamic app icon only, 56x56 with 8px radius. Do not render the right-side target app icon or any blue/red debug frame. Title is DingtalkWorkSpace, subtitle is 请求以下钉钉账号进行授权.
visualDesignTarget.accountBlock: Light #fafafa rounded 424x60 account row below the header, with real org/user avatar at left and the current account name next to it.
visualDesignTarget.permissionBlock: Light #fafafa rounded 424x300 permission panel titled 可授权的权限. Product rows use a blue checked checkbox, bold product name, gray count such as 12 项, gray description text, and a right chevron for expandable details.
visualDesignTarget.selectionBehavior: All recommended groups are checked by default. Product checkbox toggles the entire group; expanding a product reveals individual scope checkboxes.
visualDesignTarget.manageTip: Under the permission panel, show the existing 个人数据授权管理 link text for later review or revoke.
visualDesignTarget.buttons: Bottom two-button layout: left outlined white 拒绝 button, right primary blue 同意并授权 button, same height and rounded pill style.
visualDesignTarget.mobile: On narrow screens the same hierarchy must remain visible without text overlap; buttons share the available width.

terminalTuiTarget.usageGuide: Interactive dws auth login --recommend starts the terminal authorization guide only after OAuth/device login, DingTalk organization selection, login summary, and token persistence are complete. The first guide screen is 使用引导 / 选择操作 with options 直接使用CLI, 一键配置智能体应用, 手动输入应用凭证. 直接使用CLI continues directly to authorization setup; manual credentials collect AppKey/AppSecret for subsequent runtime credential use and do not replace the already selected login organization.
terminalTuiTarget.authorizationScopeSelector: After the usage guide, the TUI presents 授权范围选择 with 推荐授权 and 全部授权. 推荐授权 is the default safe path and calls pat.batch_plan with recommend=true. 全部授权 replans the selected product domains with recommend=false so PAT core/server risk handling decides pending/grant behavior.
terminalTuiTarget.loginRecommendSelector: After the login is already complete and the usage/scope guides have run, interactive dws auth login --recommend presents 选择要授权的业务域 with all product domains selected by default.
terminalTuiTarget.rowFormat: Product rows follow the MGDone terminal target: selected checkbox, left-aligned product code, Chinese product name, and short operation summary separated by a plain hyphen, such as approval   审批 - 审批实例，审批模板，审批任务管理.
terminalTuiTarget.controls: TUI must support space toggle, up/down navigation, slash filter, enter confirm, and ctrl+a select all through the underlying selector component. Description text is 空格选择 回车确认 while the component renders the detailed key hints.
terminalTuiTarget.nextStep: Without --yes, after product confirmation the CLI prints/opens the complete encoded authorization URL returned by the server and waits for browser approval. With --yes, the CLI skips the product TUI and web authorization page and directly grants the service-selected safe scopes.

securityRules: Gateway derives orgId and uid from trusted token/header only.
Gateway allowlist must include openclaw and dws-cli for batch grant callers that are intended to write.
PAT core consumes the Diamond recommendation set for recommend=true and caller=auth_login_recommend; prepub Diamond currently recommends 151 low-risk scopes and excludes the 22 medium-risk scopes.
PAT core must use flow-stored scopes as the allowlist and reject any selectedScopes value outside the flow.
PAT core must only clear pending keys owned by the current flow and leave the flow pending if approve write fails after precheck, so the same authorization page can retry safely.
Critical approve/reject storage writes must fail closed with PAT_BATCH_PARTIAL_FAILURE instead of reporting success on partial persistence.
Batch flow approval must be idempotent; repeated approval or retry must not duplicate grants.
acceptanceCriteria: Prepub package/build succeeds for every changed repo: dingtalk-workspace-cli, lippi-open-mcpserver-gw, lippi-pat-core-wukong-capability, lippi-open-app-dev, and lippi-open-dev-front.
Prepub deployed versions include the current branch commits for CLI, gateway, PAT core, BFF, and frontend.
On prepub, dws auth login --recommend opens the batch authorization URL after successful login.

On prepub, interactive dws auth login --recommend first completes DingTalk login and organization selection, then displays the terminal authorization chain: 选择操作, 授权范围选择, and default-all-selected product-domain TUI before opening the authorization URL.
On prepub, dws auth login --recommend --yes does not open the authorization page and grants the service-selected recommended scopes directly.
On prepub, dws pat chmod --recommend --product calendar without --yes opens the authorization URL.
On prepub, dws pat chmod --recommend --product calendar --yes preserves the existing direct grant behavior.
On prepub, a recommended config containing a delete/recall/remove/destroy/drop scope returns warnings/skipped scopes and never grants that scope.
On prepub, approving a batch flow grants only selectedScopes and rejects scopes outside the flow.
On prepub, rejecting a batch flow writes no new grants.
On prepub, JSON mode returns structured PAT_BATCH_AUTH_PENDING with uri/openBrowser fields and does not block.
On prepub, the authorization page visual result matches visualDesignTarget and the provided screenshot style.

reviewPlan.implementationCommander: Check end-to-end CLI, gateway, PAT core integration contracts.
reviewPlan.aiNativeUiArchitect: Requested by goal but no local skill was available; use Product Design and existing frontend constraints as fallback.
reviewPlan.qinzePuaReviewer: Review security, idempotency, tests, and failure modes.
reviewPlan.muskChiefOperator: Review the product flow for speed, minimum surface area, and user clarity.
reviewPlan.computerUse: Verify prepub/front-end page only after frontend repo and build credentials are available.
